item.title: Breaking the therapeutic ceiling in inflammatory bowel disease: myth or reality?
item.description: The lancet. Gastroenterology & hepatology - Review - Despite an expanding therapeutic armamentarium, remission rates in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) appear to plateau at 30-50% after 1 year of treatment, leading to the concept of a therapeutic ce...

item.title: A novel clustering of down syndrome and coeliac disease complicated by enteritis and small intestinal strictures: a case series.
item.description: Therapeutic advances in gastroenterology - Case report - Down syndrome (DS) increases risk for autoimmune conditions, including coeliac disease (CeD). Here, we report five adults with DS and biopsy-proven CeD who developed enteritis and symptomatic small...

item.title: Role of enteral nutrition in the clinical care, management, and quality of life of patients with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Frontiers in nutrition - Enteral nutrition (EN) is essential for Inflammatory Bowel Disease treatment. This applies mainly to Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC) patients. The study evaluated the impact of EN ...
